what_it_does: This file is a captured noticeboard item that records a proposed reusable event-to-action capability made of a CC-launch node, a trigger registry, and a hook firing mechanism, intended for the graph/MCP layer and first used to auto-research newly filed board items.
observations: Tim-directed build dated 2026-06-17, decompressed from a dense transmission.
Decomposes the idea into three composable pieces: CC-LAUNCH NODE, TRIGGER REGISTRY, and HOOK.
CC-LAUNCH NODE is described as a typed composable unit with input variables and a structured output written to an address.
TRIGGER REGISTRY holds declared event-to-action rules using address patterns and output targets.
HOOK is defined as the lifecycle firing mechanism, distinct from the TRIGGER rule that rides it.
First use case is dogfood: a board item is filed, a trigger fires, a CC-launch node researches it, and findings are attached as a linked board item.
Process follows a scan-before-build law: fan-out sub-agents scan current code, produce a resource pack, then design and implement.
Status is SCOPING with scans already launched.
Relates to routing (#71), graph MCP registries, the board, and channel/clone-launch infrastructure.
Frontmatter includes id, address, type=idea, source=claude_code, state=captured, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
standouts: The explicit distinction between a hook (plumbing/lifecycle interception) and a trigger (declared registry rule).
The framing of a model launch as a typed, reusable dataflow node rather than a one-off script.
The scan-before-build process and the choice to dogfood the system on its own noticeboard.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with board:// addresses and a history transition block.
Body uses section headers and wiki-style links such as [[project-introspective-data-building]].
Follows the project's stated principles: registry-declared, composable, no hardcoding, directory-as-graph.
concerns: Status is SCOPING and the scan is running, so details may drift.
The file admits no trigger/hook registry exists today and that this still needs confirmation.
References specific model/provider names (deepseek-v4-flash/kimi) that may become outdated.
Dense transmission decompression may have lost nuance.
notes: A scoping noticeboard item that proposes a registry-driven trigger/hook/CC-launch-node substrate and plans to dogfood it with board auto-research.
questions: What exact MCP tool surface will agents use to declare triggers?
What is the address-pattern grammar for matching events?
What is the output schema for the CC-launch node's structured result?
Which existing launch infrastructure (cc_clone/supervisor) is being reused?
How will the routing (#71) layer connect triggers to hooks?
Will the implementation use dynamic-Workflow or fan-out sequencing?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to capture and scope a reusable event-to-action capability, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth and declarative configuration over hardcoded logic.
Composable typed nodes in a directory-as-graph system.
Introspective self-build: using the system to improve its own data.
Everything variables: inputs and outputs as addressable data.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a reusable, registry-driven substrate where agents can declaratively set event triggers that launch model nodes and write results back into the graph, starting with automatic research on new board items.
novelty: The explicit separation of hook from trigger, and the treatment of a near-frontier model launch as a typed, input-to-output composable node in a dataflow graph.
contribution: Contributes a scoped design decomposition, terminology (CC-launch node, trigger registry, hook), a concrete first-use scenario, and a scan-before-build process that a fused system could keep as a design/requirements note.
summary_for_embedding: A captured noticeboard idea proposing a reusable event-to-action substrate composed of a CC-launch node, a trigger registry, and hook firing mechanism, to be dogfooded by auto-researching newly filed board items.
intention_for_embedding: To scope and record a registry-driven, composable trigger-and-launch capability for the graph/MCP layer before implementation.
